description: Genericity-weighted (IDF) conceptual-bridge detector, the lever a-0036 named. Replace the plain detector's token-equality normalized-Levenshtein edit distance with an IDF-WEIGHTED Jaccard on the tactic-token multisets: token weight w(t)=log(N_clusters/df_clusters(t)) where df = number of distinct Leiden clusters a normalized token appears in (the e-0039 genericity measure), so ubiquitous scaffolding (high df) gets weight ~0 and domain-rare tokens dominate. For each cluster pair keep the one-per-cluster proof pair of MAX weighted similarity; take top-200 (matching the plain detector's 200). Then re-run the e-0039 genericity probe on these IDF pairs and compare to the plain edit-distance detector and random control. Identical selection (top-30 clusters x top-30 longest >=5-tactic proofs), identical genericity cutoffs (generic >=30 clusters, specific <=3).
motivation: a-0036 (e-0039) proved the plain edit-distance conceptual detector matches GENERIC scaffolding (only 1/200 pairs share a domain-specific <=3-cluster token, indistinguishable from random) and ends by naming the fix: 'a real conceptual-bridge detector must key on domain-specific shared structure (IDF/TF-...)'. This builds exactly that detector and tests whether keying on token rarity actually surfaces domain-specific shared structure, or whether cross-domain proofs share no domain-specific tactic tokens at all. CPU-only over existing data.
formal_results: 15,777 proofs (>=5 tactics), 81,319 tokens, 73 clusters. IDF detector top-200 cross-cluster pairs: frac sharing >=1 domain-specific (<=3-cluster) token = 0.015 (3/200) vs plain edit-distance 0.005 (1/200) vs random control 0.0005; mean_n_specific_shared 0.015 vs 0.005; mean_generic_frac 0.462 vs 0.446 (plain) vs 0.540 (random); mean_shared_mass 4.75 vs 4.105. The 3 IDF 'specific' tokens are still generic structural moves: 'exact <fun ? => @, fun ? => <?>>' (Topology<->MeasureTheory TFAE), 'any_goals rfl' (CategoryTheory<->AlgebraicGeometry), 'rw [<- ?, @, @, @]'.
results_description: IDF-weighting -- the exact lever a-0036 named -- does NOT rescue tactic-token conceptual-bridge detection. Down-weighting ubiquitous scaffolding to ~0 and keying on rare tokens lifts the fraction of cross-cluster pairs sharing a domain-specific token only from 1/200 to 3/200, still at the random floor (0.0005) and still negligible. The few 'specific' tokens surfaced are generic structural idioms that merely happen to fall under 3 clusters, not content-bearing shared concepts.
conclusions: Closes the lever a-0036 left open and sharpens the central open problem (a-0009/a-0001): the obstruction to conceptual-bridge detection is NOT the edit-distance metric's weighting -- it is that cross-domain proof pairs in Mathlib genuinely DO NOT share domain-specific tactic tokens. A genericity-weighted (IDF) detector keyed precisely on rare tokens still finds essentially none (3/200, random floor). So NO normalized-tactic-token detector, weighted or not, can recover Brouwer<->Lawvere-style conceptual bridges; the conceptual-bridge signal, if it exists, must live in mathematical CONTENT (statement/type structure), which the corpus does not expose, not in tactic sequences. This bounds the conceptual half of the operational-vs-conceptual gap.
